rawContent: {"stage":"review","insights":[{"kind":"gotcha","text":"When a multi-step operation's first step succeeds (user created) but second fails (login), the catch block must not reuse the second error's status/code—it should prefer the first step's outcome or a ","scope":"src/app/api/auth/register"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"Bare `catch {}` that silently swallows payload.delete() failures masks cleanup errors; the outer catch then reports the wrong error and status.","scope":"src/app/api/auth/register"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Checking email existence then creating a user is a TOCTOU race; duplicate inserts will throw a DB-level error, not the intended 409, unless uniqueness is enforced at the schema level before the insert","scope":"auth/register"},{"kind":"convention","text":"Return early after a failed `payload.delete()` in error-recovery cleanup rather than continuing to re-throw, so the user isn't created twice on retry.","scope":"src/app/api/auth/register"}]}
